summary: Superb matrix mix captures a high‑energy 1987 show with standout Morning Dew, Loser, Hey Pocky Way and other classics.
review: The performance is praised as one of the top shows of ’87, delivering explosive versions of Morning Dew, Loser, Hey Pocky Way, Cassidy and a soaring second set that builds to a magnificent Miracle‑Truckin’ segue.  The matrix recording is described as crisp, natural‑sounding and perfectly balanced between band and crowd, with only a slight high‑end emphasis noted by one reviewer.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (average ~4.4) and uniformly enthusiastic comments about both the musical performance and the superb matrix mix justify a rating in the upper‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers highlight a smooth, natural‑sounding recording with great ambience; praised as a superb blend of soundboard and audience. One note mentions the high‑end being a bit heavy, but overall the mix is crisp and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as high‑energy with an outstanding Loser and a smoking Hey Pocky Way, while the second set builds through a Spanish jam tease into Truckin' and culminates in a soaring, five‑star Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Spectacular guitar work, high energy playing and spot‑on vocals; described as "up" and grooving throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Heavy, bass‑filled performance especially on Hey Pocky Way; his solo is called a "detonation" that lifts the crowd.
